bio.quote: Vigmund Haldorsson is the Jarl of Rindgate — a man who has spent the last eight months refusing to give up on three hundred people who have nowhere else to go. He is weathered, practical, and deeply t

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

novice.description: Advantage (Second Chance) on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

system.description:
Skills: Cunning, Handle Animals, Crafting.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

Perk: *Practical Stores*. Once per extended rest, you can declare you packed one mundane supply item that fits your travel (food, cord, oil, simple tools).

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 200

system.description: Whenever you are in wilderness, once you are hidden from a creature, you don’t need cover or concealment to stay hidden from them.

novice.description: Whenever you are in wilderness, once you are hidden from a creature, you don’t need cover or concealment to stay hidden from them.

system.description:
Strategist. Defender. Leader. With weapon in hand, you lead allies into the maw of battle, barking out commands that inspire your fellow heroes to move faster and strike more precisely. All the while, you stand between your compatriots and death, taunting the followers of evil to best you if they can.

As a tactician, you have abilities that heal your allies and grant them increased damage, movement, and attacks, even as you leave your enemies struggling to respond.

system.description:
You have drilled with a broad array of arms and armor, and have developed techniques to optimize their use. You can use and gain the benefits of two kits, including both their signature abilities. Whenever you would choose or change one kit, you can choose or change your second kit as well. See Chapter 6: Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper.)

If both kits grant you the same benefit, you take one or the other and can’t change your choice until you finish a extended rest. (This usually means taking the higher of two bonuses.)

For example, if you take the Shining Armor and Sniper kits, you gain the following benefits overall:

  • Toughness Bonus: +12 per echelon

  • Resistance Bonus: +1

  • Melee Damage Bonus: +2/+2/+2

  • Ranged Damage Bonus: +0/+0/+4

  • Speed Bonus: +1

  • Ranged Distance Bonus: +10

  • Disengage Bonus: +1

  • You can use the Patient Shot and Protective Attack signature abilities.

Kit signature abilities have their kit’s bonuses already applied, which might require you to adjust the bonuses of the signature abilities you gain from a kit. For example, you might take the Martial Artist kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +2/+2/+2, and the Mountain kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +0/+0/+4. If you choose to use the Mountain kit’s damage bonus, then the Battle Grace signature ability from the Martial Artist kit loses the +2/+2/+2 bonus from that kit, reducing its usual 5/8/11 damage for its tier 1, tier 2, and tier 3 outcomes to 3/6/9. It then gains the +0/+0/+4 of the Mountain kit to deal 3/6/13 damage.

system.description: The Ranger kit outfits you with light armor and weapons for every challenge, letting you easily switch between melee and ranged combat. This kit provides a good balance of bonuses to defense and offense to create a hero who is a jack-of-all-trades.

novice.description: The Ranger kit outfits you with light armor and weapons for every challenge, letting you easily switch between melee and ranged combat. This kit provides a good balance of bonuses to defense and offense to create a hero who is a jack-of-all-trades.

system.description: The Mountain kit does exactly what it says on the tin. You don heavy armor and raise a heavy weapon to stand strong against your foes, quickly demolishing them when it’s your turn to strike.

novice.description: The Mountain kit does exactly what it says on the tin. You don heavy armor and raise a heavy weapon to stand strong against your foes, quickly demolishing them when it’s your turn to strike.

system.description:
The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

Focus in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain focus equal to your Milestones. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 focus.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a creature marked by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.zyd6C7phOZr6EAQ9]{Mark}), you gain 1 focus. The first time in a combat round that any ally within 10 meters of you uses a heroic ability, you gain 1 focus.

You lose any remaining focus at the end of the encounter.

Focus Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain focus outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost focus without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs focus, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited focus on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of focus equal to your Milestones.

system.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. At the start of an encounter, you can use a free triggered action to use your Mark ability against one enemy you have line of effect to, even if you are surprised. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 meters, ignoring resistance. The target can’t be moved in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.

novice.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 meters, ignoring resistance. system.description: In response to another creature communicating information to you, you can spend a Experience to determine whether that information contained any knowing lies. If so, you know what the lies are, but not what the truth is.

novice.description: In response to another creature communicating information to you, you can spend a Experience to determine whether that information contained any knowing lies. If so, you know what the lies are, but not what the truth is.

system.description: You have an encyclopedic knowledge of warfare, viewing the battlefield as a game board and seeking victory by thinking steps ahead of your opponents. You gain a skill from the lore skill group.

novice.description: You have an encyclopedic knowledge of warfare, viewing the battlefield as a game board and seeking victory by thinking steps ahead of your opponents. You gain a skill from the lore skill group.

system.description:
Your encyclopedic knowledge of the history of battle lets you apply that knowledge to current challenges. While you are present, each hero with you treats the Discover Lore project related to a war or battle as one category cheaper. This makes projects seeking common lore free, but such projects still require a extended rest activity to complete.

Additionally, if you have 24 hours or more before a combat encounter or negotiation, and you have one or more clues or rumors regarding the encounter or negotiation, you can make a Reason test as a extended rest activity. The following test outcomes apply to a combat encounter:

!

The Director tells you the number of creatures in the encounter.

@

The Director tells you the number and level of the creatures in the encounter.

#

The Director tells you the tier 2 outcome information, and when the encounter begins, all enemies are surprised.

The following test results apply to a negotiation:

!

The Director gives you three motivations, one of which belongs to an NPC in the negotiation.

@

The Director gives you one motivation for an NPC in the negotiation.

#

The Director tells you the tier 2 outcome information, and you and each of your allies gains an advantage (second chance) on tests made to influence NPCs during the negotiation.

You can make this test only once for any encounter or negotiation.

system.description: You have learned to leverage your marked foes’ psychology and goad them into acting before they’re tactically ready. Whenever a creature marked by you uses a strike that targets you or any ally within your line of effect, you can use a free triggered action to change one target of the strike to you or another ally within your line of effect. The new target must be within distance of the ability and within line of effect of the creature using it.
